warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui functions for phenol (C6H5OH) were computed with:
• Property: atom‐condensed Fukui indices (f⁺ for electrophilic attack, f⁻ for nucleophilic attack, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites.
• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”); **no density fitting** (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF RKS.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied phenol.xyz (13 atoms) taken as rigid; single-point calculations run at the neutral (N), cationic (N–1), and anionic (N+1) charge states. No geometry optimization was performed (designation: B3LYP/def2-TZVP//input geometry).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent specified).
• Electronic state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet) for the parent; cation +1 doublet and anion –1 doublet used for the N±1 states.
• Software: chemkit wrapper around PySCF 2023.12 (fchk build) for all three SCF calculations.
• Convergence & integrity: all SCF calculations converged. The integrity checks report charge-conservation warnings (see below) but flag them as ok; overall trustworthy = true.
  – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
  – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• Live log: the run was streamed to a file created by chemkit (path was displayed during execution) containing full PySCF SCF output; available on request.

Selected numerical results (atomic indices are 0-based):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 2 (C) with f⁺ = 2.1028319.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 0 (O) with f⁻ = 0.16201477.
• Arrays (in atom order O,C1-C6,H’s):
  – f⁺  = [−0.99513677, 0.39933191, 2.10283190, 1.61968060, −0.34200644, −0.28910826, 1.30797530, −0.73391780, −0.27465615, −0.91706058, −0.62991625, −0.29591677, 0.047899341]
  – f⁻  = [ 0.16201477, 0.048772551, 0.070972379, 0.070498539, 0.026704972, 0.020949414, 0.12351549, 0.082321145, 0.088147788, 0.081105985, 0.080487046, 0.09349577, 0.051014152]
  – Dual = f⁺ − f⁻ as reported in the JSON.

The headline value returned below is the largest electrophilic Fukui index, f⁺ = 2.1028319 (carbon atom 3 in 1-based indexing).
